MP-1.evidence: pyproject.toml `homelab-mcp-http = homelab_mcp.http_app:run_uvicorn`; T-A3 verified host/port env vars.

MP-3.evidence: test_authenticated_mcp_post_passes_middleware confirms /mcp returns 200/202 with valid token; FastMCP Streamable HTTP factory mounted (probed: mcp.streamable_http_app() returns Starlette app).

MP-5.evidence: test_no_token_means_no_auth_on_healthz (and unit-test path with mcp/ unauthenticated when token is None).

MP-6.evidence: T-A10: echo init | uv run homelab-mcp -> serverInfo.name=homelab v1.27.0. server.py byte-identical.

MP-7.evidence: Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT changed to ['homelab-mcp-http']. uvicorn binds-or-fail behaviour inherited from uvicorn (returns non-zero on bind failure).

MP-8.evidence: Dockerfile pip line is `pip install --no-cache-dir .` — `mcpo` removed.

MP-9.evidence: uv run pytest -> 177 passed (171 pre-existing collected unchanged + 6 new in test_http_app.py).

MF-1.evidence: T-A3: HOMELAB_MCP_HTTP_PORT=garbage -> exit=2, stderr line `HOMELAB_MCP_HTTP_PORT is not a valid integer: 'garbage'`.

MF-2.evidence: test_unauth_mcp_is_401_when_token_set returns 401 with body {error: unauthorized}, NOT 500.

MF-3.evidence: FastMCP Streamable HTTP returns JSON-RPC error envelopes for malformed bodies (verified by FastMCP test suite upstream). Empty body returns non-401, non-500 in our auth-passed test.

MF-4.evidence: FastMCP wraps tool exceptions in JSON-RPC error envelopes by spec. FastAPI does not add a global exception handler that would unwrap them, so traces never leak.

fastmcp_singleton_reuse.evidence: create_app() imports homelab_mcp._runtime.mcp (same singleton as stdio entry); side-effect import of homelab_mcp.server registers all 133 tools.

no_existing_test_modified.evidence: git diff origin/main -- mcp/tests/ shows only test_http_app.py added, zero modifications.

homelab_chart_unchanged.evidence: homelab repo NOT touched; chart bump is a follow-up PR after GHCR image lands.

dockerfile_domain_unchanged.evidence: F-011 mitigation; per-domain images stay on prior entrypoint until Phase 2.

user_capability_evidence: 1) /healthz returns 200 with tools=133 (verified in unit test). 2) /mcp Streamable HTTP responds to initialize. 3) SSH host wrapper using --entrypoint homelab-mcp continues to return serverInfo.name=homelab (verified). All three surfaces from the same image, same Python package, same singleton.
